description: SQS commands do not serialize input parameters into the JSON request body. Commands like get-queue-url --queue-name and get-queue-attributes --queue-url fail with QueueDoesNotExist on existing queues because parameters aren't sent. Only list-queues works (no required params). List parameter serialization also fails with SerializationException.
notes: Found by validation agent. SQS uses JSON protocol. The request body is being sent empty despite parameters being provided on the command line.
Fixed by build_member_name_map() - same root cause as validation-json-protocol-params-not-serialized in issues-04.

description: RDS commands ignore --output text and --output table flags, always outputting JSON regardless. Verified on describe-db-instances and describe-db-engine-versions. This is specific to RDS (query protocol) - other query protocol services like STS and IAM handle output formats correctly.

verify.expect: Should output text format, not JSON
Should output table format, not JSON

notes: Found by validation agent. RDS JSON output is correct, but text/table format selection is broken.
Fixed by fixup_global_flags_in_trailing_args() - --output after operation args was captured as trailing var arg.

description: Three RDS commands are not recognized by raws: add-option-to-option-group, remove-option-from-option-group, and generate-db-auth-token. The first two may be legacy commands; generate-db-auth-token is a client-side token generation utility.

verify.expect: Should be recognized as a valid command

notes: Found by validation agent. generate-db-auth-token is a commonly used command for IAM database authentication.

description: RDS describe-reserved-db-instances-offerings returns empty output from raws while AWS CLI returns 13,827 offerings. Likely a pagination issue with very large result sets.

verify.run: raws rds describe-reserved-db-instances-offerings --region us-east-1 --output json 2>&1 | head -5
verify.expect: Should return offerings data, not empty output

notes: Found by validation agent. May be related to auto-pagination handling for responses with many pages.

description: ECS deploy and monitor-express-gateway-service are custom AWS CLI commands not in the botocore service model. raws returns 'Unknown operation' for these. Also applies to cloudformation package command.

verify.expect: Custom CLI command - may not be implementable from botocore models

notes: Found by validation agent. These are AWS CLI-specific commands that don't correspond to API operations. Low priority since they require custom implementation outside the botocore model.
